  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Brazing was performed without a nitrogen purge, resulting in black cupric oxide scale inside the refrigerant lines."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop work immediately.",
    "Cut out the contaminated sections of the copper tubing.",
    "Flush the system to remove any loose scale.",
    "Re-braze the joints while maintaining a continuous low-pressure nitrogen purge (1 to 2 PSI)."
  ],
  "rationale": "Brazing copper refrigerant lines without a nitrogen purge allows oxygen to react with the heated copper, forming black scale (cupric oxide) inside the tubing. This scale will flake off when refrigerant flows, clogging the expansion valve (TXV/piston), filter drier, and damaging the compressor. The contaminated piping must be cut out and replaced under a proper nitrogen purge before evacuation and charging can proceed.",
  "references": [
    "EPA Section 608",
    "ACCA Standard 5 (HVAC Quality Installation Specification)",
    "Manufacturer Installation Instructions (Brazing & Evacuation Protocols)"
  ]
}
